summary: Selected Train active/history transition as the next UI regression expansion surface. The triage compared Write promotion, Home protocols, Train, Patterns, and localization layout, then queued the existing expansion slice with a narrow Train Batch 2 scope. No product code or XCUITest code changed.

contract_status_changes.before: The next UI regression expansion slice was blocked because no concrete surface was selected.
contract_status_changes.after: Train active/history transition is selected and scoped as Batch 2: seed one planned Train session, resolve it through a visible Train-tab action, and assert it leaves active Today and appears in History.

contract_status_changes.before: Some workflow text still described Owlory as lacking a batched regression suite even though TodayContinueRegression exists.
contract_status_changes.after: Workflow docs now describe the current Today Continue regression batch honestly and warn not to treat it as broad app-wide UI regression coverage.

residual_risks: This was a triage slice only; no Train XCUITest implementation was added.
The selected Train batch will still need deterministic seed support, stable Train accessibility identifiers if missing, and a passing make ui-regression run.
Screenshot, device, and TestFlight proof are out of scope for the queued Train regression batch.
next_recommended_reason: The next surface is now selected and scoped; the queued implementation slice should add Train active/history transition regression coverage without broadening into other surfaces.
